The vines are located right at the end of the piney z trail, That's the green trail marker on the park maps. When the trail ends and opens up to the train tracks, there are vines on either side. Access: Public.

These trees are in a city-owned park. Harvest time is fall. There are LOTS of good pecans out there on the ground. Be careful where you park. We parked at the corner of Ty Ty and Olivia and were harassed by a beligerent and insane homeowner.

This is a large lot with a gravel parking lot and a few trees. There are a couple pecan trees in this lot.

The trees are on the decline due to root compaction; the yield is still pretty good, but the nuts are smaller than many of the other trees around town
